instructions.instruction: Always read the YAML template at assets/templates/ready-for-refinement.yaml before generating any ticket data file.
Fetch the Jira ticket using jira_get_issue with fields including issuelinks to discover linked incidents.
Fetch every linked incident ticket and read all its comments — comments contain the investigation findings used to populate the context.
original_snippets: Comments on the incident ticket contain the investigation findings — read them all.

instructions.instruction: Never leave a required YAML field empty or as the placeholder "".
original_snippets: Never leave a required field empty or as the placeholder ""

instructions.instruction: conditions_of_satisfaction.must must have at least one item; acceptance_criteria must have at least two items.
original_snippets: conditions_of_satisfaction.must must have at least one item
acceptance_criteria must have at least two items

instructions.instruction: Validate the YAML data file with validate-ticket.sh and fix all errors before generating the markdown output.
original_snippets: Fix any reported errors before proceeding to markdown generation.

instructions.instruction: After generating the markdown, run validate-ticket.sh again with the --markdown flag to confirm structure.
Store outputs at <journal-dir>/YYYY/MM/YYYY-MM-DD-PROJ-NNN-ready-for-refinement.{yaml,md} using today's date.
original_snippets: Use today's date for YYYY-MM-DD. The journal-dir is the current project root.

instructions.instruction: Omit the Linked Incident line from the markdown header if no incident is linked to the ticket.
Report the YAML path, markdown path, validation result, and a one-paragraph summary when the workflow is complete.
original_snippets: report: Path to the YAML data file, Path to the markdown output file, Validation result, A one-paragraph summary
